panels.title: 😈 Snarky vs. Nice
panels.infobox: Track which choices you have made. Checking a row = you have resolved this encounter. Snarky choices generally unlock missable items and XP bonuses.

entry_All.name: Mary (Houton pub – after killing the giant rat)
entry_All.snarky: You get laid.
entry_All.nice: Mary considers you a milksop and you sleep alone.
entry_All.notes: One of the game's first joke choices.

entry_All.name: Bannafeet the Trow (Houton)
entry_All.snarky: Bannafeet sends you on a fake errand to the Well – empty room, 200 XP for your trouble. He then reveals the real quest on your return.
entry_All.nice: Bannafeet skips the runaround and gives you the quest info directly. Miss the 200 XP bonus.
entry_All.notes: Snarky is worth doing just for the XP, but the outcome is the same.

entry_All.name: Daurrad (after clearing the Bugbear Cairn)
entry_All.snarky: You receive 5 Silver Pieces.
entry_All.nice: You receive 75 Silver Pieces.
entry_All.notes: Be nice here.

entry_All.name: Ketill (tied up in Houton Forest jail)
entry_All.snarky: He stays tied up. No help.
entry_All.nice: He fights alongside you but abandons you partway through — still draws enemy fire while he lasts.
entry_All.notes: Nice is better. Even brief ally support helps.

entry_All.name: Chubnik's ferry (Neversdale Forest)
entry_All.snarky: Keep being snarky: price drops from 15 SP → 10 SP → free. The ride costs nothing.
entry_All.nice: Pay 15 Silver Pieces for the ferry crossing.
entry_All.notes: This choice carries forward: if snarky to Chubnik, Piskey charges 105 SP later; if nice, Piskey is free.

entry_All.name: Piskey's ferry (northern Neversdale) — downstream effect of Chubnik choice
entry_All.snarky: 105 Silver Pieces (penalty for being snarky to Chubnik earlier).
entry_All.nice: Free (reward for being nice to Chubnik earlier).
entry_All.notes: Not a direct choice here — result depends entirely on how you treated Chubnik.

entry_All.name: Ketill (tied up in northern Neversdale, just before the Fnarf boss fight)
entry_All.snarky: He stays tied up. Fight Fnarf alone.
entry_All.nice: He fights alongside you temporarily before betraying you and running. Still draws attack while present.
entry_All.notes: Nice is worth it — having a distraction during Fnarf is genuinely helpful.

entry_All.name: Ogan's mum (Houton — return visit after Neversdale events)
entry_All.snarky: Anything other than being nice twice yields 300 XP, but no token.
entry_All.nice: Be nice TWICE (tell her you avenged Ogan): 200 XP + Token: Rabbit's Foot (+1 Luck).
entry_All.notes: Must visit her TWICE, being nice both times. Easy to miss.

entry_All.name: Bodb chain (multiple Bodbs in Kirkwall leading to Light Fairy tune)
entry_All.snarky: Being snarky to each Bodb earns his respect — he leads you to the next one.
entry_All.nice: Being nice causes each Bodb to brush you off until you give him some sass.
entry_All.notes: Must be snarky throughout the entire chain to progress. The one time snarky is the only option.

entry_All.name: Bodb on The Hidden Bluff / Finn's Farm (north of Kirkwall)
entry_All.snarky: 150 Silver Pieces.
entry_All.nice: Nothing.
entry_All.notes: Be snarky. Another unusual case where snarky pays.

entry_All.name: Farmer Finn (asking for the Magic Bell)
entry_All.snarky: Finn hands over the Bell immediately — but the Farmer's Daughter scene plays out anyway.
entry_All.nice: Finn makes you do a Scarecrow-clearing quest first, then gives you the Bell. Same result in the end.
entry_All.notes: Both paths lead to the Conner subplot. Snarky saves time; nice has a slightly richer scene.

entry_All.name: Conner (The Fat Lute pub)
entry_All.snarky: He starts swinging. DO NOT fight back — let Conner beat the Bard to near-death. The Farmer's Daughter leaves with him and you get 300 XP. Bonus: summon the Rat in the pub for 50 SP + 275 XP.
entry_All.nice: Conner goes to Finn's peacefully. Less satisfying outcome; miss the Rat pub trick.
entry_All.notes: Tip: Even if nice, summon the Rat in the pub afterward for the 275 XP bonus regardless.

entry_All.name: Mell (Highland Park — gatekeeper to the electrified energy corridors)
entry_All.snarky: He simply waits for you to be nice. You cannot proceed without him.
entry_All.nice: He guides you through the energy-gated park sections.
entry_All.notes: Snarky is a dead end here. Must be nice.

entry_All.name: Gower (Mountain Pass entrance — offers warming brew)
entry_All.snarky: Pay 100 Silver Pieces for the brew.
entry_All.nice: The brew is free.
entry_All.notes: Gower appears again at the end — that choice matters too (see below).

entry_All.name: Hrafn the Viking King (Burial Chamber, Frozen Tombs)
entry_All.snarky: Provoke him repeatedly — Hrafn becomes a powerful temporary ally and leads your assault on the Draugr.
entry_All.nice: He ignores you entirely.
entry_All.notes: Must be repeatedly snarky to trigger the ally event. Being nice accomplishes nothing here.

entry_All.name: Dolyn (trapped behind ice wall in Frozen Tombs)
entry_All.snarky: If unrelentingly snarky, Dolyn runs away after being freed and is killed by a trap. Loot his body for Token: Ring of Tara (+2 Rhythm). WARNING: Dolyn being dead prevents you from getting the White Book of Rhydderch later (Ch.12).
entry_All.nice: Dolyn unlocks doors, grants access to Token: Golden Coronet (+2 Charisma), and gives 880 XP before departing. Keeping him alive lets you get the White Book of Rhydderch token in Ch.12 (worth +50 max mana).
entry_All.notes: IMPORTANT LONG-TERM CHOICE. Being nice to Dolyn is strongly recommended — the White Book of Rhydderch is far more valuable than the Ring of Tara.

entry_All.name: Gower (Mountain Pass — returns after you emerge from the Tombs)
entry_All.snarky: Keep the Casgair sword. Get 330 XP.
entry_All.nice: Return the Casgair to Gower — your sword reverts to what you had before. Receive Token: Badge of Wind (+1 Dexterity).
entry_All.notes: Tough call. If you don't need the Token, keeping the Casgair (a strong weapon) is reasonable.

entry_All.name: Cathbad the Druid (Mountain Tower Grove — boss encounter)
entry_All.snarky: Cathbad's pride is wounded and he dismisses his Kunaltrow guards. Fight Cathbad one-on-one. He drops 5 Adderstones.
entry_All.nice: Cathbad brings ALL surrounding Kunaltrow into the fight with him. Much harder.
entry_All.notes: Be snarky. Fighting Cathbad alone is significantly easier.

entry_All.name: Reynard Brothers (West Dounby — extortion quest)
entry_All.snarky: Escorted out the door. Quest unavailable.
entry_All.nice: They explain the task (get 50 Silver from Donnan). Then be nice to Donnan → nice to Caltir → confront brothers → earn Tune: Knocker + 1100 XP. Bonus: summon the Knocker in front of the brothers for another 1100 XP.
entry_All.notes: Must be nice throughout this entire questline to get the Knocker tune.

entry_All.name: Finstown Armory door guards (Ch.9 — asking your identity)
entry_All.snarky: Fight begins immediately.
entry_All.nice: Fight begins, but the guards exchange an amusing dialogue first.
entry_All.notes: Purely cosmetic difference. Fight is unavoidable either way.

entry_All.name: Prison Camp gate guard (Finstown)
entry_All.snarky: Nothing. Gate stays closed.
entry_All.nice: Gate opens, earn 1100 XP. Speak to the refugee inside for Instrument: Finn's Lute (3 summons, 400 mana).
entry_All.notes: Be nice. The Lute is a meaningful upgrade.

entry_All.name: Refugee girl (Finstown prison camp — calls you a chiseler)
entry_All.snarky: Kicked in the nuts. 451 XP.
entry_All.notes: Snarky technically gets more XP, but only by 51. The guide seems amused by this.

entry_All.name: Village Elder (Finstown — after defeating Silkbeard)
entry_All.snarky: Gratitude of nothing.
entry_All.nice: Gratitude of nothing.
entry_All.notes: Irrelevant choice — same outcome regardless.

entry_All.name: Apologize Man (MacRath's Dungeon — Room 1)
entry_All.nice: (Not mentioned — snarky is the recommended path here.)
entry_All.notes: Be as snarky as possible. He is the man from Houton who wronged you earlier.

entry_All.name: Trow by Hatcher's house (Stromness)
entry_All.snarky: Tune: Upgraded Gouger + 880 XP.
entry_All.nice: Warnings about the Finfolk in the Mines. No tune.
entry_All.notes: Snarky wins here. The Upgraded Gouger is the better reward.

entry_All.name: Trow on the east pier (Stromness)
entry_All.snarky: Nothing.
entry_All.nice: Tune: Upgraded Elemental + 880 XP.
entry_All.notes: Be nice to this one. Together with the Hatcher Trow, you need one snarky and one nice response.

entry_All.name: Culainn the Firbolg (Firbolg Mines — trapped miners quest)
entry_All.snarky: 1000 Silver Pieces immediately, then 3300 XP. If snarky AGAIN on the follow-up offer, get 2000 Silver Pieces total but NO Ego Sword.
entry_All.nice: Rescue quest: gather scattered miners → 2200 XP + Ego Sword (powerful, long-range) + 2200 Silver Pieces.
entry_All.notes: Nice is the better outcome overall (Ego Sword is excellent), but snarky gives more raw Silver.

entry_All.name: Firbolg Bannock (Finfolk Caverns — trapped Firbolg in SW section)
entry_All.snarky: He runs to a clearing and is mauled by Finfolk. If you let him die: 550 XP. Miss the Armbands + Bodyguard Tune.
entry_All.nice: Lead Bannock safely to the raft: he gives you Firbolg Armbands (Strength +3). Then report to Olav: 1650 XP + Tune: Upgraded Bodyguard.
entry_All.notes: Be nice. The Strength token and Bodyguard upgrade are much better than 550 XP.

entry_All.name: Olav (Finfolk Caverns — long-winded man north of the caverns)
entry_All.snarky: Interrupt him repeatedly: he stops rambling and gives you the Firbolg Armbands task.
entry_All.nice: Let him finish speaking without interrupting: 850 XP + Token: Lightning Stone (+3 Dexterity).
entry_All.notes: Nice (letting him finish) is the better outcome. The Lightning Stone is one of the strongest Dex tokens.

entry_All.name: Chosen One (Obligatory Lava Level, Finfolk Caverns exit)
entry_All.snarky: He runs across the rock bridge to open the chest — dies on the way back. Loot his body for Tune: Upgraded Behemoth.
entry_All.nice: Convince him to sit down: 2200 XP + Instrument: Harp of Ages (4 summons, 600 mana).
entry_All.notes: Tough call — Harp of Ages is excellent (best mana capacity alongside Ossian's Lute), but the Upgraded Behemoth is required to complete the tune collection.
